title: Psychotria bealananensis
taxonomy: Psychotria bealananensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant is native to the tropical forests of New Guinea, specifically in the island of New Guinea. It is a type of shrub or small tree that grows in the understory of the forest, with a habit of climbing onto other vegetation. The classification of this plant is within the genus Psychotria, which is a group of plants commonly known as wild coffee or joewood. Further classification within the species is not well-documented.

intro: Psychotria bealananensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the Bealanana Psychotria or Psychotria bealananensis. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria bealananensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Psychotria bealananensis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems.
